longDescription: The Systems, Man, and Cybernetics (SMC) committee works on Brain-Machine Interfaces using EEG technology and Machine Learning. They are currently developing a 128-electrode EEG cap and a robotic hand controlled by brain waves. The committee pushes the boundary between human cognition and machine control.
name: Systems, Man & Cybernetics

projects.name: 128-Electrode EEG Cap
projects.description: A high-density EEG cap capable of capturing detailed brain activity for real-time brain-machine interface applications.

projects.name: Brain-Controlled Robotic Hand
projects.description: A robotic hand that responds to EEG-detected brain signals, enabling gesture control through thought alone.

description: Works on EEG-based brain-machine interfaces and ML-powered robotic hand control.
